ddls:
CREATE TABLE "job_postings" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job posting */,
  "position_category" TEXT /* Position or category of the job */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* More specific title of the job position */,
  "job_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the job responsibilities and requirements */,
  "required_skills" TEXT /* JSON array of required skills for the job */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the job is situated */,
  "location_coordinates" TEXT /* Geographical coordinates (latitude and longitude) for the job location */,
  "pay_type" TEXT /* Pay range or type for the job */,
  "salary_min" TEXT /* Minimum salary range */,
  "salary_max" TEXT /* Maximum salary range */,
  "salary_currency" TEXT /* Currency for the salary range */,
  "employment_type" TEXT /* Type of employment (e.g., full-time, part-time, contract) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or division within the company */,
  "posted_date" TEXT /* Date when the job was posted */,
  "posted_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the hiring manager who posted the job */,
  "application_link" TEXT /* Link for candidates to apply for the job */,
  "active_status" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the job posting is active */,
  "application_deadline" TEXT /* Date by which applications must be submitted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_posted_by FOREIGN KEY ("posted_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job posting being applied for */,
  "candidate_name" TEXT /* Name of the candidate applying for the job */,
  "candidate_email" TEXT /* Email address of the candidate */,
  "candidate_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the candidate */,
  "resume_url" TEXT /* URL to the candidate's resume */,
  "cover_letter_url" TEXT /* URL to the candidate's cover letter */,
  "application_source" TEXT /* Source from which the application was received (e.g., job board, LinkedIn, referral) */,
  "interview_scheduled" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if an interview is scheduled */,
  "interview_date" TEXT /* Date of the scheduled interview */,
  "hiring_manager_notes" TEXT /* Notes from the hiring manager about the candidate */,
  "recruiter_id" INTEGER /* User ID of the recruiter managing the application */,
  "application_date" TEXT /* Date the application was submitted */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the application (e.g., submitted, reviewed, hired) */,
  "application_status_change_date" TEXT /* Date when the application status was last changed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_recruiter_id FOREIGN KEY ("recruiter_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES job_postings ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_type" TEXT /* Type of user (e.g., hiring manager, recruiter, administrator) */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "user_email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "user_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or division within the company */,
  "active_status" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "communication" (
  "communication_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each communication */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user (hiring manager or recruiter) */,
  "communication_date" TEXT /* Date of the communication */,
  "communication_type" TEXT /* Type of communication (e.g., email, phone call, in-person meeting) */,
  "communication_notes" TEXT /* Notes about the communication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("communication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communication_application_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_id") REFERENCES applications ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communication_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
